channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABNT20 KNHC 221145
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
800 AM EDT Wed Jul 22 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Bertha, located over the northern Gulf of America.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

$$
Forecaster Reinhart
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Bertha (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...TROPICAL STORM WATCHES EXTENDED WESTWARD ALONG THE EXTREME SOUTHWESTERN LOUISIANA AND UPPER TEXAS COASTS... As of 7:00 AM CDT Wed Jul 22 the center of Bertha was located near 29.5, -88.5 with movement W at 5 mph. The minimum central pressure was 997 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 50 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Bertha
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...TROPICAL STORM WATCHES EXTENDED WESTWARD ALONG THE EXTREME SOUTHWESTERN LOUISIANA AND UPPER TEXAS COASTS...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Public Advisory Number 12a
item.description:
Issued at 700 AM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 221145
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Bertha Intermediate Advisory Number 12A
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
700 AM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026
 
...TROPICAL STORM WATCHES EXTENDED WESTWARD ALONG THE EXTREME
SOUTHWESTERN LOUISIANA AND UPPER TEXAS COASTS...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 700 AM CDT...1200 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.5N 88.5W
ABOUT 45 MI...75 KM NE OF THE MOUTH OF THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER
ABOUT 60 MI...100 KM S OF PASCAGOULA MISSISSIPPI
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...50 MPH...85 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 270 DEGREES AT 5 MPH...7 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...997 MB...29.44 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
The Tropical Storm Watch has been extended westward to Sargent, 
Texas.
 
SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
A Tropical Storm Warning is in effect for...
* Okaloosa/Walton County Line in Florida to Morgan City, Louisiana
* Metropolitan New Orleans and Lake Pontchartrain
 
A Tropical Storm Watch is in effect for...
* West of Morgan City, Louisiana, to Sargent, Texas
* Lake Maurepas
 
A Tropical Storm Warning means that tropical storm conditions are
expected somewhere within the warning area within 36 hours.
 
A Tropical Storm Watch means that tropical storm conditions are
possible within the watch area, in this case within the next 24 to
48 hours.
 
For storm information specific to your area, including possible
inland watches and warnings, please monitor products issued by your
local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 700 AM CDT (1200 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Bertha was
located near latitude 29.5 North, longitude 88.5 West. Bertha is
moving toward the west near 5 mph (7 km/h), and this general motion 
is expected to continue through Friday with a little acceleration. 
On the forecast track, Bertha will move near or along the northern 
Gulf Coast during the next couple of days.
 
Data from a NOAA Hurricane Hunter aircraft indicate that maximum 
sustained winds remain near 50 mph (85 km/h) with higher gusts. 
Gradual weakening is forecast over the next several days.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 105 miles (165 km)
from the center. A wind gust to 49 mph (79 km/h) was recently 
reported at the Pensacola Naval Air Station.
 
Dropsonde data from the NOAA aircraft indicate that the minimum 
central pressure is 997 mb (29.44 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
Key messages for Tropical Storm Bertha can be found in the Tropical
Cyclone Discussion under AWIPS header MIATCDAT2 and WMO header
WTNT42 KNHC.
 
WIND:  Tropical storm conditions will continue spreading across
portions of the northern Gulf Coast within the warning area today.
Tropical storm conditions are possible within the watch area in
Louisiana by tonight and along the upper Texas coast by Thursday or 
Thursday night.
 
STORM SURGE:  The combination of storm surge and the tide will
cause normally dry areas near the immediate coast to be flooded by
rising waters moving inland from the shoreline. The water could
reach the following heights above ground somewhere in the indicated
areas if the peak surge occurs at the time of high tide...
 
Mouth of the Mississippi River, LA to MS/AL Border...2-4 ft
Port Bolivar, TX to Mouth of the Mississippi River, LA...1-3 ft
MS/AL Border to Okaloosa/Walton County Line, FL...1-3 ft
Lake Pontchartrain...1-3 ft
 
The deepest water will occur along the immediate coast.
Surge-related flooding depends on the relative timing of the surge
and the tidal cycle, and can vary greatly over short distances. For
information specific to your area, please see products issued by
your local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
For a complete depiction of areas at risk of storm surge inundation,
please see the National Weather Service Peak Storm Surge Graphic,
available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?peakSurge.
 
RAINFALL: Bertha is expected to produce total rainfall amounts of 1
to 4 inches with isolated higher totals to 6 inches through Thursday
along the central to the western Gulf Coast from western Florida to
southern Texas. This rainfall may produce isolated flash flooding,
especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Bertha, please see the National Weather Service Storm Total Rainfall
Graphic available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and
the Flash Flood Risk graphic at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF: Swells generated by Bertha will continue spreading westward
along the northern Gulf Coast during the next few days. These swells
are likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip current
conditions. Please consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 1000 AM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Berg
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Advisory Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026 
419 
WTNT22 KNHC 220836
TCMAT2
 
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  12
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  88.3W AT 22/0900Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST OR 270 DEGREES AT   4 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  998 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  45 KT WITH GUSTS TO  55 KT.
34 KT....... 60NE  90SE  60SW  40NW.
4 M SEAS.... 60NE 120SE  60SW  60NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  88.3W AT 22/0900Z
AT 22/0600Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  87.9W
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 29.4N  89.4W...ON THE COAST
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT... 50NE  80SE  60SW  40NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0600Z 29.3N  91.4W...ON THE COAST
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT...  0NE  80SE  60SW  30NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 29.3N  93.9W...OVER WATER
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT...  0NE  50SE  50SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 29.5N  96.3W...POST-TROP/INLAND
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 29.4N  88.3W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 22/1200Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 22/1500Z
 
$$
FORECASTER PAPIN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Discussion Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 400 AM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026 
000
WTNT42 KNHC 220845
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Discussion Number  12
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
400 AM CDT Wed Jul 22 2026
 
Since the previous advisory, Bertha's appearance has gone downhill, 
with the deepest convective activity separating further south from 
the low-level circulation which is now mostly exposed offshore of 
the coast of Alabama. An Air Force Reserve reconnaissance mission in 
completing its last pass through Bertha found peak flight level 
winds of 47 kt, and a minimum central pressure that has risen 
slightly to 998 mb. While a dropsonde launched in the southeast side 
of the circulation did find a 500 m layer mean wind of 54 kt, a 
standard reduction from this layer still yields a slightly reduced 
intensity of 45 kt for this advisory. Given Bertha's current 
appearance, this could be generous. 
 
A combination of aircraft, radar, and satellite fixes indicate that 
Bertha is moving due west at 270/4 kt. The primary steering feature 
resulting in Bertha's short-term motion is a mid-level ridge that 
has been sliding slowly eastward from the southern Plains to become 
positioned north of the tropical storm. This evolution should result 
in Bertha maintaining a westward heading over the next 24-36 h, 
though continued sheared bursts of deep convection forming south of 
the center may still act to tug it south of this steering motion. 
One thing worth keeping an eye on is the possibility a new center 
may try to reform under these convective bursts in the 24-36 h 
time-frame like what is depicted in some of the hurricane-regional 
models, though this is not reflected in the NHC track forecast at 
this time. The latest track forecast is nearly on top of the 
previous, but just a little faster, roughly in between the latest 
Google DeepMind (GDMI) and HFIP Corrected Consensus (HCCA) 
solutions. 
 
Bertha's appearance, the presence of 25-30 kt of northeasterly 
vertical wind shear, and land interaction are likely to result in 
slow weakening over the next 24 h or so. After that point, Bertha's 
future likely depends on if the circulation remains along the Gulf 
coast, or if it attempts to reform further south under the 
convective bursts in the northwestern portion of the Gulf. In the 
former scenario, Bertha's circulation could open up into a trough 
like in the 00z ECMWF and HWRF solutions. In the latter case, a new 
center could reform further south and attempt to reorganize like in 
the HAFS and GFS solutions. For what it's worth, the Google DeepMind 
ensemble seems to be roughly split between these two possibilities. 
For now, the NHC intensity forecast does not stray far from the 
previous one, showing slow weakening before Bertha moves fully 
inland over Texas in 48 h, becoming post-tropical and dissipating 
shortly thereafter. This intensity forecast is roughly in the middle 
of the guidance envelope and closest to the GDMI solution. 

 
Key Messages:
 
1. Tropical storm conditions are expected to continue along portions 
of the northern Gulf Coast from the western Florida Panhandle to 
southeastern Louisiana, including the Metro New Orleans area, 
through tonight. Tropical storm conditions are possible for portions 
of the southwestern coast of Louisiana tonight into Thursday.
 
2. A Coastal Flood Warning is in effect for the Mississippi and
Louisiana coastlines, where moderate coastal flooding is expected.
 
3. Interests elsewhere in extreme western Louisiana and along the
Upper Texas Coast should monitor the progress of this system, as
additional watches or warnings could be required for portions of
that area later today. 
 
4. Isolated areas of flash flooding remain possible through early 
Friday along the central to the western Gulf Coast from western 
Florida to southern Texas.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  22/0900Z 29.4N  88.3W   45 KT  50 MPH
 12H  22/1800Z 29.4N  89.4W   40 KT  45 MPH...ON THE COAST
 24H  23/0600Z 29.3N  91.4W   35 KT  40 MPH...ON THE COAST
 36H  23/1800Z 29.3N  93.9W   35 KT  40 MPH...OVER WATER
 48H  24/0600Z 29.5N  96.3W   30 KT  35 MPH...POST-TROP/INLAND
 60H  24/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Papin
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Wind Speed Probabilities Number 12
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026 
000
FONT12 KNHC 220841
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  12           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0900Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 29.4 NORTH...LONGITUDE 88.3 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 45 KTS...50 MPH...85 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        06Z WED AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       06Z WED 18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 06Z SAT 06Z SUN
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 06Z SAT 06Z SUN 06Z MON
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
GFAM 290N 870W 34 98   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)   X(98)
 
GULFPORT MS    34  4   1( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
STENNIS MS     34  4   1( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
BURAS LA       34 61   7(68)   X(68)   X(68)   X(68)   X(68)   X(68)
 
GFAM 280N 890W 34 15   X(15)   X(15)   X(15)   X(15)   X(15)   X(15)
 
NEW ORLEANS LA 34  3   8(11)   1(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)
 
HOUMA LA       34  4  19(23)   1(24)   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)
 
GFAM 280N 910W 34  1  10(11)   1(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)
 
BATON ROUGE LA 34  X   2( 2)   1( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
MORGAN CITY LA 34  1  14(15)   2(17)   X(17)   X(17)   X(17)   X(17)
 
LAFAYETTE LA   34  X   2( 2)   2( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
NEW IBERIA LA  34  X   4( 4)   4( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
 
GFAM 280N 930W 34  X   2( 2)   8(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)   X(10)
 
LAKE CHARLES   34  X   X( X)   4( 4)   1( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
CAMERON LA     34  X   2( 2)  21(23)   1(24)   X(24)   X(24)   X(24)
CAMERON LA     50  X   X( X)   3( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
KOUNTZE TX     34  X   X( X)   2( 2)   2( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
PORT ARTHUR TX 34  X   X( X)   5( 5)   2( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
GALVESTON TX   34  X   X( X)  14(14)  11(25)   X(25)   X(25)   X(25)

HOUSTON TX     34  X   X( X)   1( 1)   7( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)

GFAM 280N 950W 34  X   X( X)   4( 4)   5( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)
 
HIGH ISLAND TX 34  X   X( X)   7( 7)   4(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)

PORT O CONNOR  34  X   X( X)   1( 1)   8( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)
 
$$
